description: Semantic ontology defining concepts, entities and relationships within the EU AI Act Compliance Management System.

namespace.name: Remote Regulatory Verification Infrastructure™
namespace.domain: EU AI Act Compliance Management System™

Organization.definition: A legal organization responsible for operating AI systems and implementing compliance activities.

RRVI_System.definition: A structured system used to organize AI governance obligations, controls, documents and evidence.

AI_System.definition: A machine-based system capable of generating outputs such as predictions, recommendations, decisions or content.

AI_Use_Case.definition: A specific business scenario where an AI system is used.

Module.definition: A structured EU AI Act governance area containing requirements, controls and documentation.

Document.definition: A uniquely identified, versioned and managed compliance document.

Evidence.definition: Objective information demonstrating that a compliance activity has been performed.

Risk.definition: Potential impact, obligation or uncertainty related to AI usage.

Risk_Category.definition: EU AI Act risk level assigned to an AI use case.

Human_Oversight.definition: Measures ensuring appropriate human supervision of AI system operation.

Incident.definition: An event affecting AI system compliance, safety, availability or integrity.

Regulation.definition: External legal or regulatory framework mapped to compliance obligations.

ontology_rules: Every AI System must have an operational owner.
Every AI Use Case must have a risk classification.
Every compliance document must belong to a module.
Every evidence object must support a compliance statement.
No orphan compliance objects are permitted.
